qa.overall_assessment: All three blocking objections from iteration 1 are correctly resolved. OBJ-1: AC11 is now properly classified as C1 (Could Have) forward-proofing with verified pre-fix analysis showing all 4 rules do not fire. OBJ-2: M2 now contains explicit binding language requiring that only the dangerous-token grep for line 743 uses COMMAND_CONTEXT_STRIPPED; the workflow-path grep must remain on raw $COMMAND. OBJ-3: AC13 uses the real incident command (codex exec with killall+claude in prompt body), verified to trigger rule 715 before the fix. All 5 dimensions pass. Spec approved for dev handoff.

objection_resolution_verdicts.resolution_claimed: AC11 reclassified to C1 forward-proofing with NOTE explaining pre-fix behavior (all 4 rules do not match before fix). The AC is classified C1_forward_proofing in acceptance-criteria JSON and ticket now says 'before OR after fix' in WHEN clause.
objection_resolution_verdicts.qa_verification: Verified via grep and Python regex simulation against all 4 rule patterns. Results: rule 715 — no match; rule 722 — no match; rule 743 compound — match on (rm|mv)\s but NOT on workflow-path, so compound evaluates False; rule 751 — no match (pattern (^|[;|&]\s*)rm\s fails because '; # rm' has '#' interceding between ';' and 'rm', not whitespace). All 4 rules confirmed non-firing before fix.
objection_resolution_verdicts.technical_detail: The rule 751 pattern (^|[;|&]\s*)rm\s requires: start-of-line OR control operator THEN optional whitespace THEN 'rm ' with trailing space. The command segment '; # rm is not needed here' has '; ' then '#' then ' rm' — the '\s*' after the semicolon matches the space but then encounters '#' where 'r' is expected. Pattern fails. grep -qE exit code 1 confirmed.
objection_resolution_verdicts.evidence: grep -qE '(^|[;|&]\s*)rm\s' on command returns exit code 1. Python regex re.search PATTERN_751, cmd = False. All 4 rules independently verified as non-matching.

objection_resolution_verdicts.resolution_claimed: M2 requirement now contains explicit binding text: 'For line 743's compound condition specifically: ONLY the dangerous-token grep (rm|mv)\s is applied against COMMAND_CONTEXT_STRIPPED; the workflow-path grep (workflow-[^/]*.json|.claude/todos/) MUST remain on raw $COMMAND.' Multiple artifacts encode this constraint.
objection_resolution_verdicts.qa_verification: Verified in three artifacts: (1) ticket-20260522-080646-A.md M2 text contains the explicit split language with ONLY and MUST — confirmed by direct read. (2) context-20260522-080646-A.json requirement.scope.included states 'for line 743 compound condition, only the dangerous-token grep applies against COMMAND_CONTEXT_STRIPPED; the workflow-path grep MUST remain on raw $COMMAND (binding per M2)'. (3) development_approach.strategy states 'CRITICAL for line 743: only the first grep (dangerous-token check) uses COMMAND_CONTEXT_STRIPPED; the second grep (workflow-path check) MUST remain on raw $COMMAND per M2 binding'. (4) Technical Hints now cross-references 'per binding requirement M2'.
objection_resolution_verdicts.evidence: Read ticket M2 text at lines 72-73 verbatim. Read context JSON requirement.scope.included. All three artifacts consistently encode the binding constraint in M2.

objection_resolution_verdicts.resolution_claimed: AC13 now uses real incident command: codex exec "The hook documentation says killall and claude processes are monitored". NOTE added with pre_fix_behavior and incident_evidence fields.
objection_resolution_verdicts.qa_verification: Verified via grep -qE '(killall|pkill)\s+.*(happy|claude|docker)' on the AC13 command string. grep exit code 0 (match = BLOCKED). Pattern analysis: 'killall' appears early in the string; '.*' greedy-matches the middle text; 'claude' appears at end of the quoted string — all on one line (grep is line-local so .* does not cross newlines). Rule 715 fires before fix. COMMAND_CONTEXT_STRIPPED would strip the quoted string argument to 'codex' (a non-shell binary), leaving only 'codex exec' in the executable view — rule 715 would not fire after fix. This is the correct expected behavior.
objection_resolution_verdicts.evidence: grep -qE rule 715 on AC13 command returns exit code 0. Python re.search(PATTERN_715, cmd_ac13) = True. incident_evidence field in acceptance-criteria JSON confirmed correct.

out_of_scope_observations.observation: qa_objection_revision.objections_addressed[0].codex_input for OBJ-1 says 'Codex BLOCKER: using same codex command in both AC11 and AC13 creates redundancy' — the old AC11 used python3 -c (not codex exec), so the codex_input description slightly mischaracterizes the original structure. This is a documentation artifact in BA's internal process record and does not affect spec correctness. The resolution itself (classify AC11 as C1 forward-proofing, use real codex command in AC13) is correct.

Line 751's docker exclusion (!echo "$COMMAND" | grep -qE 'docker\s+rm\s') stays on raw $COMMAND after the fix, while the dangerous-token grep (rm\s) moves to COMMAND_CONTEXT_STRIPPED. This is safe: for a genuine 'docker rm container_id' command, the stripper would strip 'rm container_id' as non-shell arguments to the non-shell binary 'docker', leaving only 'docker' in COMMAND_CONTEXT_STRIPPED — so (^|[;|&]\s*)rm\s would not match the stripped view anyway. The docker exclusion is belt-and-suspenders. This was not flagged by BA and does not require spec revision — the behavior is correct by construction. Noted here for dev awareness.

Codex exec invocation was itself blocked by line 715 (prompt body contained 'killall' and 'claude' in the QA challenge text). This is a 3rd independent live reproduction of the exact false-positive the spec is designed to fix. Self-review was substituted per graceful fallback protocol.

codex_consult.feedback_summary: Codex exec invocation was blocked by hook line 715 — the Bash command containing the prompt body ('...killall...claude...') triggered the exact false-positive this spec is designed to fix. This is live confirmation #3 of the bug. Error: BLOCKED: Killing happy/claude/docker processes is forbidden.
codex_consult.feedback_incorporated: self-review substituted per graceful fallback protocol. Five adversarial questions addressed: (1) Is rule 751 analysis for AC11 correct? Yes — confirmed by grep exit code 1, # intercedes between ; and rm. (2) Does line 751's docker exclusion need split treatment? No — stripping docker's arguments removes 'rm' from the stripped view anyway, so the outer dangerous-token check correctly fails for 'docker rm'. (3) Is .* in rule 715 line-local? Yes — grep is line-by-line, confirmed. (4) Is M2 binding language sufficient? Yes — binding text in M2, not just Technical Hints, with explicit ONLY/MUST language. (5) Any new drift after revision? No — all three resolutions are clean and consistent across ticket, context JSON, and acceptance criteria JSON. One minor internal documentation inaccuracy in codex_input noted as OOS observation.

evidence_quality.summary: All factual claims verified. Lines 715, 722, 743, 751 patterns confirmed by direct source read. AC11 pre-fix pass verified by grep (exit code 1 for all 4 rules). AC13 triggers rule 715 verified by grep (exit code 0). Live block observed in BA session (codex exec). A 3rd live block occurred during QA's own codex invocation attempt. BA's python regex simulation results match QA's independent grep verification.

scope_alignment.summary: Scope covers exactly 3 files. M1-M10 map to all 7 user requirement clauses (R1-R7). C1 (Python comment stripping) is correctly Could Have. W1-W4 correctly exclude full AST parsing, other hooks, Layer 1.A-E, and absolute bans. No universal user scope claim was narrowed or renamed. User's verbatim requirement 'rewrite the hook's matching core to distinguish command-form context from string-content context' is fully captured in M1-M10.

investigation_completeness.summary: BA identified 4 specific rules causing false positives by line number. Performed codex consultation (4 BLOCKERs + 5 MAJORs all incorporated). Documented live reproduction during BA session. All investigation deliverables present: line numbers, patterns, code snippets, live block evidence, disposition records.

affected_file_accuracy.summary: hooks/pretool-bash-safety.sh: exists, 1050 lines, all 4 patterns at stated lines confirmed. hooks/lib/bash_context_strip.py: correctly identified as absent (new file). hooks/tests/test_bash_safety_context.py: correctly identified as absent (new file). hooks/tests/ directory exists with other test files. All component_chain entries match read source.

spec_text_vs_execution_drift.summary: All three OBJ resolutions verified as clean: AC11 classification C1_forward_proofing is consistent across ticket and JSON. M2 binding text is explicit and present in the requirement (not just Technical Hints). AC13 command is confirmed real-incident-reproducible via grep. No remaining drift detected across the 16 ACs.
